originalTitle: Somaliland opens Jerusalem embassy after Israel's recognition of its independence
title: Somaliland opens Jerusalem embassy after Israel's recognition of its independence
content: The move comes six months after Israel became the first country to recognise the breakaway East African state.

originalTitle: Recovery of Ebola patients offers rare moments of joy at epicentre of outbreak
title: Big Smiles in Congo! People Are Getting Better!
content:
Hello, my amazing friends all across Africa! Your kid-friendly news reporter is here with some very important news. Today, we have a story that will make your heart feel warm and happy. It comes from a beautiful country in Africa called the Democratic Republic of Congo. Let's talk about it!

Hello from Africa!

I am your special news friend, coming to you from our wonderful continent, Africa! We have so many amazing animals, beautiful lands, and brave people here. Sometimes, we hear about things that make us feel a little sad. But today, I want to tell you about something good. It is a story about hope and big smiles! We are going to talk about a sickness called Ebola. You might have heard about it. It can make people very unwell. But guess what? Many people who get this sickness are now getting better! That is very happy news indeed!

What is Ebola? (A Quick Look)

So, what exactly is Ebola? It is a sickness caused by a very, very tiny germ. This germ is so small you cannot see it with your eyes. It makes people feel tired and can give them a fever. Sometimes, it makes their tummy hurt. When someone gets Ebola, they need a lot of help. They need special doctors and nurses to take care of them. These helpers are very brave. They wear special clothes to keep themselves safe while they help others. *Did you know?* The word “Ebola” comes from a river in Africa! It was named after a river because that is where the sickness was first found a long, long time ago.

Where is This Happening?

This story is happening in a country called the Democratic Republic of Congo. We often call it DRC for short. It is a very big country right in the middle of Africa. It has big forests and many different animals. In some parts of DRC, people have been getting sick with Ebola. It has been a very hard time for many families. But even when things are tough, there is always hope. And that hope is shining brightly right now! *Did you know?* The Democratic Republic of Congo is the second-largest country in Africa! It's super big, even bigger than some countries put together.

Sad News First, Then Good News!

At first, many people were getting sick, and it made everyone very worried. Sadly, some people even died because of this sickness. This made everyone feel very, very sad. Losing someone we love is always hard. More than 170 people had died because of this tiny germ. That is a big number of people. But here is the good news! Even with all that sadness, doctors and nurses kept working hard. They did not give up. And now, their hard work is showing wonderful results!

Hooray! People Are Getting Well!

This is the most exciting part! Many people who were sick with Ebola are now feeling better. They are strong again! Can you imagine the big smiles on their faces? And the happy tears from their families? When someone recovers from Ebola, it is a huge celebration! They get to go home and hug their families. It is a moment of pure joy. These moments of happiness are like sunshine breaking through the clouds after a big rainstorm. Imagine seeing a friend who was very sick, suddenly run and play again! That's how happy people feel when they see someone recover from Ebola. Each person who gets better gives hope to everyone else. *Did you know?* When someone recovers from Ebola, they often get a certificate or a special card to show they are healthy and free from the sickness! It's like a superhero badge!

How Do They Get Better?

So, how do people get better from Ebola? It is not by magic! It is because of very hard-working doctors, nurses, and other health helpers. They give the sick people special care and sometimes special medicine. These brave helpers work in special places called treatment centres. They make sure the sick people get enough to drink and eat. They help them feel comfortable. They watch over them day and night. Sometimes, the body just needs time and a lot of help to fight off the tiny germ. The doctors and nurses are there to give that help. They are like guardians for the sick people, protecting them and helping them heal. *Did you know?* Doctors and nurses who care for Ebola patients wear special suits that cover them from head to toe! This keeps them safe from the tiny germs. They look a bit like astronauts!

Meet the Heroes!

Who are the heroes in this story? Well, first, the people who got sick and fought hard to get better are big heroes! They showed great strength and courage. They never gave up hope. And then there are the doctors, nurses, and other health workers. They are heroes too! They work very long hours. They are far away from their own families sometimes, all to help others. They are so kind and so brave. These heroes are teaching us that even when things are very scary, we can work together. We can help each other. Their smiles are just as important as the medicine they give. They bring hope to everyone in DRC.

What Can We Do to Stay Safe?

Even though we are talking about happy news, it is good to remember how to stay safe from germs. We can all be heroes in our own homes and schools! The most important thing is to wash your hands often! Use soap and water, and wash for as long as it takes to sing 'Happy Birthday' twice! This washes away those tiny, unseen germs. Also, try not to touch your face, especially your eyes, nose, and mouth. These are like little doors for germs to get into your body. And if someone is not feeling well, it's good to give them a little space. *Did you know?* Washing your hands properly is one of the best ways to stop germs from spreading! It's like having a superpower to keep everyone healthy!

A Big Hope for Everyone!

The news that people are recovering from Ebola in the Democratic Republic of Congo is wonderful. It means that even the toughest challenges can be overcome with hard work, bravery, and kindness. This news gives great hope to all the people in DRC, and all of Africa. It shows that we are strong. It shows that we care for each other. And it shows that together, we can make big, positive changes. Let's send our big smiles and happy thoughts to the brave people and helpers in the Democratic Republic of Congo. They are showing us all how important it is to never give up. Keep spreading those good vibes!
summary: Happy news from the Democratic Republic of Congo, where many people who were sick with Ebola are now getting better, thanks to brave doctors and nurses, bringing joy and hope to families.
whyMatters: This is important because it shows that even when a sickness is scary, people can get well with help, and it gives everyone hope that we can beat tough challenges together.

originalTitle: Nigerian army frees widow of ex-general who died in captivity
title: Nigerian army frees widow of ex-general who died in captivity
content: Maj Gen Rabe Abubakar and his wife Amina were abducted in north-west Nigeria at the end of last month.

title: Tehran selling deal with US as victory – but for Iranians it was necessity

originalTitle: Chalobah replaces injured Livramento for World Cup
title: A Big Change for England's World Cup Team!
content:
Hello, Football Fans!

Hi everyone! It's your kid-friendly news reporter here, bringing you some exciting news from the big world of football! We know many of you love to kick a ball around, right? Well, today we have a story about a very important football game called the World Cup!

What is the World Cup?

Imagine all the best football teams from different countries playing against each other. That's the World Cup! It's like the biggest football party ever. Teams play to see who is the very best in the whole wide world. It's super exciting! Did you know? The World Cup only happens once every four years! That's a long, long time to wait, which makes it extra special. Everyone loves to watch it!

Sad News for Tino

Now, let's talk about one of the teams, England. They have many fantastic players. One of them is a player named Tino Livramento. He is a super fast and strong player. He plays in a special spot called a defender, which means he helps stop the other team from scoring goals! But oh dear, we have some sad news about Tino. He got a boo-boo on his leg. It's called a calf injury. That means his leg is hurting, and he needs to rest and get better. It's super sad when a player gets hurt, especially before a big game like the World Cup. Tino worked very hard to be part of the England team. We all wish him a speedy recovery! Get well soon, Tino!

Why Do Players Get Injured?

Sometimes, when players run very fast or kick the ball very hard, their muscles can get a little bit ouchy. An injury means a part of their body is hurt and needs to heal. Just like when you fall and scrape your knee, big football players can also get hurt. When a player gets an injury, they need to take a break from playing. They need to rest their body so it can heal. This is very important so they can play again safely later. We want all our players to be strong and healthy!

A New Player Steps In!

Because Tino needs to rest and heal his leg, he cannot play in the World Cup right now. But don't worry! A big football team like England always has many amazing players. So, another super talented player will take his spot. This new player's name is Trevoh Chalobah! Yay for Trevoh! He also plays in a special spot called a defender. He is very good at stopping goals and helping his team. It's a big moment for him! Did you know? Teams have lots of players, not just the ones you see on the field at the start of the game. They have a whole squad of talented people ready to help!

Who is Trevoh Chalobah?

Trevoh is a strong and fast player, just like Tino. He plays for a club called Chelsea, which is another famous football team. Now, he gets to wear the England jersey and play for his country in the World Cup! How exciting is that? It's a big dream for many footballers to play for their country. Trevoh must be feeling very happy and proud. He will work super hard to help England do their best in the World Cup games. We are all cheering for him!

Playing for Your Country

Imagine wearing a shirt with your country's flag on it. That's what it feels like for these players. When they play for England, they are playing for all the people in England. They want to make everyone proud. It's not just about kicking a ball. It's about teamwork, trying your very best, and never giving up. These players train very hard every day to be super strong and super skilled. They are heroes to many kids, just like you! Did you know? Many children all over Africa love to play football! It’s a wonderful way to have fun, make friends, and stay healthy. Maybe one day you will play for your country too!

The Big World Cup Dream

The World Cup is a time when people from all over the world watch football. Families gather together, friends cheer, and everyone gets excited. It's a wonderful time to celebrate sportsmanship and fun. Even though Tino can't play, he will still cheer for his team. And Trevoh will play with all his heart. That's what being a good sport is all about! We learn that even when things are tough, we can always find a way to keep going and support each other.

What We Learn from Football

Football teaches us many good things. It teaches us to be strong, to work together, and to be kind to our teammates. It also teaches us to be brave and to never give up, even when we face challenges. So, let's all wish Tino a speedy recovery and send lots of good luck to Trevoh and the whole England team for the World Cup! We can't wait to watch all the exciting games! Keep playing and keep smiling, everyone!
summary: Tino Livramento got a boo-boo on his leg, so Trevoh Chalobah will now play for England in the big World Cup football games!
whyMatters: This is important because every team needs healthy players to play their very best, and now England has a new helper to make sure their team is strong.

originalTitle: Tuchel's England deal includes performance clause
title: Tuchel's England deal includes performance clause
content: Thomas Tuchel's England contract extension is subject to a performance clause, says FA chief executive Mark Bullingham.

originalTitle: Up to 15,000 England fans head to Dallas for World Cup opener

content: England fans have had a longer wait than most for their 2026 World Cup campaign to get under way. But now supporters are starting to arrive in Dallas.
